pattern: Reading a BST in-order always yields its values sorted, so rebuilding a height-balanced tree is the same subproblem as turning a sorted array into a balanced BST.
hints: A BST's in-order traversal is already sorted -- collect it and forget the original shape entirely.
From a sorted list of values, picking the middle element as a subtree's root keeps the two halves close in size.
Recurse on the left slice and right slice around that middle index to build both subtrees; any consistent middle choice yields a valid balanced BST.

edge_inputs.description: zig-zag chain alternating left and right children

stress.description: random BST built by inserting a shuffled permutation, serialized level-order
stress.note: distinct values, random insert order produces varied starting shapes
